title: Chevron Houston Marathon returns to downtown under ideal January conditions
content_md:
Runners gathered in downtown Houston on Sunday, January 19, 2025 for the Chevron Houston Marathon, one of the largest marathons in the United States and a qualifier for both the Boston Marathon and the U.S. Olympic Marathon Trials.

The race started and finished at the George R. Brown Convention Center, winding through downtown, Midtown, the Heights, and Memorial Park on a flat course that is popular with runners chasing personal-best times. Weather on race morning was cool and calm, conditions long considered ideal for distance running in the city.

Crowd support along Heights Boulevard and through Memorial is a trademark of the event, with front yards and neighborhood corners becoming impromptu aid stations and cheering sections. The companion Aramco Houston Half Marathon ran simultaneously on an abbreviated route.

After the finish, participants gathered in the convention center's post-race food hall for the hot broth, grilled cheese, and oversized medals that have become annual traditions at the Chevron-sponsored event.

title: Brazos Bend 50 brings ultrarunners to state park trails
content_md:
Trail runners converged on Brazos Bend State Park on Saturday, December 13, 2025 for the Brazos Bend 50, Trail Racing Over Texas's winter ultramarathon at the park, which sits about 45 minutes southwest of Houston.

The event offered 25K, 50K, and 50-mile distances on a looped course of packed dirt and crushed limestone, winding past hardwood forests, Spanish moss, and the levees that border the park's lakes. The course is widely regarded as one of the flattest trail races in Texas, making it a popular choice for first-time ultra runners.

Brazos Bend State Park is well known for its resident alligator population, and runners frequently reported spotting the reptiles along the levees during daylight hours. Volunteers staffed aid stations at regular intervals around the loop, and the early-morning start under headlamps has become a signature image of the event.

Trail Racing Over Texas hosts multiple races at Brazos Bend throughout the year. Post-race tacos at the finish-line tent are a long-running tradition among the ultra running community in southeast Texas.

title: Bayou City Classic 10K opens Houston's spring road-racing season
content_md:
The Bayou City Classic 10K drew runners to Sam Houston Park on Saturday, March 15, 2025, kicking off another spring racing season along Houston's downtown bayou corridor.

The out-and-back course ran along Allen Parkway and the Buffalo Bayou greenway, climbing less than 50 feet across its 10-kilometer distance. The flat profile and downtown skyline views on the return leg make it a frequent recommendation from local running clubs for those stepping up from the 5K distance for the first time.

Volunteers staffed aid stations along the course, and finishers were greeted at the line with kolaches, a Central-European pastry that has become a Houston-area staple and, for many runners, a post-race tradition. The event is part of the broader springtime running calendar along the Buffalo Bayou corridor, which in recent years has become one of the most heavily used recreational running areas in the city.

A companion 5K was offered for runners preferring a shorter distance, and the event organizers supported a walk category open to all paces.
